---
title: "Use colorblind-friendly palette for visualization"
date: 2022-01-22
image: colorblind_palette.jpg
description: "Use friendly colors in a graph with ggplot2"
categories:
  - R
  - colors
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 6, fig.height = 3.8,
                      fig.align = "center", fig.retina = 3,
                      out.width = "85%", collapse = TRUE)
```


![](colorblind_palette.jpg)

## Gray colors 

```{r}
### gray colors
cb <- c("gray", "lightgray", "darkgray", "black")
barplot(1:4, col = cb)
```
## Friendly colors

```{r}
## Use color blind-friendly palette:
cbp1 <- c(
  "#999999", 
  "#E69F00", 
  "#56B4E9", 
  "#009E73",
  "#F0E442", 
  "#0072B2", 
  "#D55E00", 
  "#CC79A7"
)
barplot(1:8, col = cbp1)
```
```{r}
### friendly colors
cbPalette <- c(
    "#000000", 
    "#999999", 
    "#E69F00", 
    "#56B4E9", 
    "#009E73", 
    "#F0E442", 
    "#0072B2", 
    "#D55E00", 
    "#CC79A7"
)
barplot(1:8, col = cbPalette)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

theme_set(
    theme(
        text = element_text(size = 10, family = "Arial"),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
        # axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.3, "lines"),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "Black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 10)
    )
)

friendly_colors <- c(
  "#4C72B0", 
  "#55A868", 
  "#C44E52", 
  "#8172B2", 
  "#CCB974", 
  "#64B5CD"
)

# Create example data frame
df <- data.frame(
    group = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"),
    value = c(10, 20, 15, 25, 30, 35)
)

# Create plot with friendly colors
ggplot(df, aes(x = group, y = value, fill = group)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = friendly_colors)

```
